global.title: Home solar in rural America: a storm just took out your power, now what?
global.summary: There’s no shortage of advice on how to size a home solar and battery system, but most of it assumes a suburban home with city services and outages measured in hours, not days. global.title: The Genesis GV90 looks huge charging next to the Kia EV9
global.summary: The GV90 is set to arrive as the biggest, most prestige Genesis electric SUV to date. With its official debut coming up, the flagship EV was spotted charging alongside a Kia EV9, revealing its true size.

global.title: Waymo taps fleet giant Element to scale its robotaxi service
global.summary: Waymo has tapped fleet management company Element to help keep its robotaxis charged, maintained, and on the road as the company expands into more cities.

global.title: BYD’s new magnetic EV device lets you control your car on the go
global.summary: BYD is about to introduce a new detachable magnetic smart device that lets you control your vehicle remotely at the push of a button.

global.title: Rivian lays off hundreds, under 2% of staff, a week after R2 launch
global.summary: Rivian laid off hundreds of workers on Tuesday, or less than 2% of its workforce, just one week after it began deliveries of its all-important R2 SUV. global.title: Hyundai is closing in on Chevy as the #2 EV brand in the US
global.summary: Hyundai is on track for its sixth straight year of record sales in the US. In the EV market, Hyundai has its sights set on Chevrolet as it looks to become the second-best-selling brand behind Tesla.

global.title: Toyota’s new EVs are catching on as registrations jump 225% in April
global.summary: With three fully electric SUVs now on sale, Toyota’s EV registrations surged 225% in April.

global.title: Kia’s electric van delivers more driving range in the real world at 260 miles
global.summary: It’s no wonder the PV5 is flying off dealer lots. With more than enough driving range, fast charging, and a high-tech, spacious interior, Kia’s electric van may be the ultimate people mover.

global.title: Tesla ‘Robotaxis’ are not crashing because they are not running
global.summary: Tesla didn’t report a single at-fault “Robotaxi” crash in the latest NHTSA autonomous-vehicle data — its only fresh incident was a Model Y getting rear-ended while stopped, clearly the other driver’s fault.
